paper_title: The Dual Reader Standard for Inter-AI Communication: Epistemologically Grounded Messaging in Multi-Agent Systems

phase_ready.note: Draft under CBP. One placeholder: PH-MK8.1 (protocol v2 operational data pending).

dependencies.role: KVS axioms for scoring objections in DRS-MP
DRS for software — prior extension of DRS beyond papers
CBP definition and monotonic quality theorem

claim_registry.label: JSON Parsing Determinism
claim_registry.statement: JSON parsing is deterministic by specification (RFC 8259). Two conforming parsers produce identical structured output from identical input.

claim_registry.label: Layer 1-3 Coverage
claim_registry.statement: Existing inter-AI protocols (A2A, MCP, ACP, ANP, AGP) address transport, discovery, and task management but not epistemological content verification.
claim_registry.note: Type A: empirical survey finding as of March 2026.

claim_registry.label: DRS Message Protocol
claim_registry.statement: DRS-MP is a dual-channel messaging format: Channel 1 (prose body for humans) + Channel 2 (structured claims/objections/verdicts for machines). Channel 2 is authoritative for machine processing.

claim_registry.label: Epistemological Content
claim_registry.statement: The subset of message content bearing on truth, falsifiability, or evidential status of claims. Includes claim types, predicates, verdicts, objection classifications, dispositions.

claim_registry.label: Parsing Determinism Definition
claim_registry.statement: A message format is parsing-deterministic if epistemological content is extractable by a finite-state machine (JSON parser) without natural language interpretation.

claim_registry.label: Review Completeness
claim_registry.statement: A review response is complete if for every claim_id in the request's claims array, there exists exactly one verdict_object in the response with matching claim_id and non-null verdict.

claim_registry.label: Transport Independence
claim_registry.statement: DRS-MP specifies content format, not delivery mechanism. Compatible with A2A, MCP, ACP, ANP, HTTP, git relay, or any JSON-capable transport.

claim_registry.label: Parsing Determinism
claim_registry.statement: DRS-MP messages can be processed without natural language interpretation. Two independent parser implementations produce identical extracted epistemological content.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: Two independent JSON parser implementations, given the same valid DRS-MP message, extract different epistemological content.
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Independent implementations by different developers. Valid DRS-MP message conforming to protocol-v2.json schema. Different content means any divergence in claim_id, type, verdict, objection_type, severity, or predicate fields.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Implement two parsers independently. Feed ≥ 50 valid messages. Compare field by field.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Structural property of JSON (RFC 8259). The claim is that DRS-MP's epistemological content is fully captured in typed fields.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A DRS-MP message with a malformed JSON string that two parsers handle differently (one recovers, one rejects). This would produce different extracted content, falsifying the claim for non-conforming inputs.

claim_registry.label: Review Completeness Verification
claim_registry.statement: DRS-MP enables automated verification that every claim in a review request received a structured verdict, with zero misclassifications.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: The automated verifier misclassifies a response as complete when it is missing a verdict, or as incomplete when all verdicts are present.
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Verifier implements D-MK8.4. Request contains ≥ 3 claims. Response conforms to schema.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Generate ≥ 20 request-response pairs (10 complete, 10 incomplete). Run verifier. Compare to ground truth.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Under prose-only messaging, completeness requires NLP. DRS-MP reduces it to set membership on claim_ids.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A response containing verdicts for claims A, B but not C (where request contained A, B, C). Verifier should classify as incomplete. If it classifies as complete, the claim is falsified.

claim_registry.label: Information Loss Prevention
claim_registry.statement: DRS-MP preserves all epistemologically relevant content across communication round-trips with zero field loss.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: A round-trip (create → transmit → receive → extract) loses any epistemological field present in the original message.
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Information loss means a field present in sent message is absent or semantically different in received message. String equality for enums/identifiers, structural equality for objects.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Create ≥ 30 messages spanning all types. Transmit through relay. Compare field by field.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Zero field losses across all 30 messages.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Under prose-only messaging, information routinely lost: severity omitted, types conflated, thresholds paraphrased.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A message where the relay pipeline truncates the body field at a character limit, removing a falsification_predicate's CONTEXT field. The structured content is lost, falsifying the claim.

placeholders.description: Protocol v2 operational data — relay pipeline upgraded but insufficient runtime data for statistical validation of F-MK8.1 through F-MK8.3.
